agntux-core.tagline: The AgntUX orchestrator. Triages action items and queries your knowledge store.
agntux-core.description:
Installs first; everything else hangs off it. Maintains your `~/agntux/`
knowledge store, triages action items per your `user.md` preferences,
and keeps `_index.md` files in sync as ingest plugins write new data.

supported_prompts.purpose: First-run interview and schema bootstrap. Creates ~/agntux/user.md and the tenant schema.
Edit your AgntUX profile — preferences, glossary, identity, sources, action-worthy/noise rules.
Capture per-plugin instructions. With a {plugin-slug} runs the install-time / on-demand teach interview.
Daily action-item digest. Recommended scheduled task at Daily 08:00.
Review or edit the tenant schema. Bare form re-walks; `review {slug}` re-reviews a contract; `edit` drives a user-initiated change.
Cross-plugin sync alias. `/agntux-core:sync {plugin-slug}` re-dispatches to the per-plugin sync command.
Catch-all for natural-language queries — entity lookups, time-window questions, topic queries, meeting prep, status edits.
Daily pattern detection over recently-resolved action items. Recommended scheduled task at Daily 16:00.

ui_components.name: triage
ui_components.title: Action item triage
ui_components.purpose: The primary inline UI. Shows priority-sorted open action items with snooze / dismiss / done buttons.

ui_components.name: entity-browser
ui_components.title: Entity browser
ui_components.purpose: Browse and pivot between people, companies, projects, and topics in your knowledge store.

notes-ingest.tagline: Turn your local Markdown notes into an AgntUX knowledge store.
notes-ingest.description:
Watches a local notes directory (Obsidian vault, Markdown folder, plain
text files) and extracts people, companies, projects, and topics as
AgntUX entities. Action items surface deadlines, follow-ups, and open
questions so nothing falls through the cracks.
data_ingested: Markdown (.md) and plain-text (.txt) files in the configured notes directory
People mentioned by name (meeting notes, introductions, action items)
Companies and organisations referenced in notes
Projects and codenames from the user's glossary

supported_prompts.purpose: Run a notes-ingest pass now (manual) or on schedule. Recommended scheduled task at Daily 09:00.

entity_subtypes.description: Individuals named or mentioned in notes (meeting attendees, action-item owners, references).
Organizations referenced in notes — customers, vendors, partners.
Named workstreams or codenames (often resolved via user.md → # Glossary).
Recurring themes, products, contracts, or research areas surfaced in notes.

action_classes.description: An informational signal extracted from a note (decision, fact, status update).
A note carrying an explicit due date.
A note flagging an open question or pending follow-up.
Escape hatch for items that don't fit the above; requires reason_detail.

proposed_schema.cursor_semantics: Filesystem mtime in milliseconds (epoch); start-of-run timestamp advances the cursor to avoid races with files modified during the run.
proposed_schema.source_id_format: Absolute file path under the configured notes directory.
